text: Artificial intelligence is becoming a bigger part of our daily lives, from recommending what to watch online to helping doctors diagnose diseases. However, ethics in AI is a growing concern. One major issue is bias—AI systems can reflect unfairness present in their training data, leading to discriminatory decisions. For example, a hiring tool might favour certain groups because of biased historical data. To ensure fairness, developers must use diverse data sets. Privacy is another key issue, as AI often collects personal information. Transparency means users should understand how AI makes decisions. Ultimately, responsible AI development requires careful attention to these ethical challenges.
